unit.name: Early 3D learning methods: render-to-2D, voxels, GANs, and octrees
unit.description: Covers the first practical deep models for 3D recognition and generation, beginning with multi-view CNNs and progressing to voxel and octree methods.
unit.summary: The first deep-learning workaround for 3D recognition was not truly 3D-native: render the shape from multiple viewpoints, run a 2D CNN on each image, and fuse the features with view pooling, thereby reusing strong ImageNet-pretrained image models [ts=2330s][ts=2354s][ts=2362s][ts=2391s]. Wu notes that this strategy worked well for classification but depended on projection quality and became brittle for noisy or incomplete geometry, motivating more native 3D methods [ts=2445s][ts=2460s][ts=2471s]. Voxel CNNs and 3D GANs then treated shape generation and classification as volumetric learning problems, benefiting from simple 3D convolution and occupancy-style losses but suffering from wasted computation in empty space and coarse resolution [ts=2508s][ts=2550s][ts=2582s][ts=2648s]. Octrees improved efficiency by recursively allocating fine cells near surfaces and coarse cells elsewhere, letting models reach higher effective resolution within the same memory budget [ts=2650s][ts=2710s][ts=2726s].

key_points.text: Multi-view CNNs solve 3D recognition by rendering many views, encoding each with a 2D CNN, and pooling across views.
Multi-view methods leverage large 2D pretrained models but require reliable projection from 3D to 2D.
Voxel-based deep models apply 3D convolution and can support both generation and classification.
3D GANs extend adversarial learning from pixels to voxels and can also support controllable 3D-to-2D generation.
Octrees recursively partition space so high resolution is used mainly near object surfaces.

kp_catalog.name: Multi-view and voxel-based 3D deep learning
kp_catalog.description: Early neural approaches that either render 3D shapes into multiple images for 2D CNNs or directly process voxel grids with 3D convolution.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because multi-view and voxel-based 3D deep learning clarifies early neural approaches that either render 3D shapes into multiple images for 2D CNNs or directly process voxel grids with 3D convolution, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: 3D datasets and AI+geometry task space
kp_catalog.description: The data and problem formulations that enabled 3D learning, including large object datasets, scanned scene data, and generative/discriminative/multimodal tasks.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because 3D datasets and AI+geometry task space clarifies the data and problem formulations that enabled 3D learning, including large object datasets, scanned scene data, and generative/discriminative/multimodal tasks, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
